  that detect CUDA → MPS → CPU in priority order

- Update all empty_cache() calls across 7 files to use the
  centralized helper (supports torch.mps.empty_cache)
- vram_budget.py: detect unified memory on macOS for VRAM budget
- profiler.py: report MPS device name in profiling output
- scheduler.py: graceful fallback when MPS has no free-mem query
- setup_worker_env.sh: install PyTorch from conda pytorch channel

commit.message:
fix(game): prevent freeze from blocking broadcasts + WebPubSub timeouts

Root cause: emitDistributed() awaited in the tick loop blocked the entire
game simulation when WebPubSub REST calls were slow or failing. The Azure
SDK's default 3-retry policy meant a single failed sendToUser took ~7s,
freezing all game ticks for that duration.

Changes:
- Non-blocking broadcasts: scheduleBroadcast() fires and forgets with a
  latest-wins pattern so the tick loop never blocks on network I/O
- WebPubSub retry disabled: maxRetries=0 since stale snapshots are
  worthless in a real-time game
- 2s abort timeout on sendToUser to cap worst-case latency
- Upgrade webPubSubSku default Free_F1 -> Standard_S1 (Free tier exhausts
  20K msgs/day in ~3 min of gameplay)
- Set apiMinReplicas default 0 -> 1 to prevent cold-start disconnections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace datetime.UTC class-attr usage with UTC alias in OTP and password reset routes (#63)

## Bug

`datetime.UTC` is a **module-level** constant added in Python 3.11. It
is **not** a class attribute of `datetime.datetime`.

All three new auth files import `from datetime import datetime` and then
call `datetime.now(datetime.UTC)`, which tries to access `UTC` as a
class attribute — raising:
```
AttributeError: type object 'datetime.datetime' has no attribute 'UTC'
```

This means **every** call to `POST /auth/login/otp/request`, `POST
/auth/password-reset/request`, and `POST /auth/password-reset/confirm`
returned HTTP 500 at runtime.

## Fix

Import `timezone` from `datetime` and use `datetime.now(timezone.utc)`
in all three files.

## Files changed
- `app/api/routes/authentication/login_otp.py` (2 occurrences)
- `app/api/routes/authentication/password_reset_request.py` (1
occurrence)
- `app/api/routes/authentication/password_reset_confirm.py` (1
occurrence)

Closes #62

---------

* fix: bump-build tests fail in CI due to CI=true env skip

The bump-build.mjs script checks process.env.CI and skips with
'Skipping build bump (CI mode)' when CI=true. GitHub Actions always
sets CI=true, so all 5 bump-build tests were silently skipping the
actual bump logic and failing on assertions.

Fix: override env in execSync calls with CI='' and SKIP_BUILD_BUMP=''
so the script actually runs during tests.

Root cause: Brady's commit 344bb2b added the CI skip guard to
bump-build.mjs but didn't update the test to account for it.

Overhaul code runner: Pyodide for Python, Godbolt for compiled, native for HTML/JS

Python:
- Runs entirely in the browser via Pyodide (WASM) loaded from CDN
- Persistent hidden iframe — Pyodide loads once (~10 MB), reused for all runs
- Auto-installs imported packages via loadPackagesFromImports (numpy, pandas,
  scipy, matplotlib, sympy, sklearn, PIL, networkx, cryptography, pydantic, ...)
- Matplotlib plots captured as PNG and displayed as images in the panel
- 60 s timeout

HTML/SVG:
- Rendered in sandboxed iframe with allow-scripts + allow-pointer-lock
- Three.js, Babylon.js, WebGL, canvas games all work via CDN script tags

JavaScript/JSX:
- Sandboxed iframe with console.log/warn/error capture via postMessage
- 5 s timeout

Compiled languages (via Godbolt Compiler Explorer — free, no auth, CORS OK):
- C++ → GCC 15.2 (g152)
- C   → GCC 15.2 (cg152)
- Go  → gc 1.26.0 (gl1260)
- Rust → rustc 1.94.0 (r1940)
- Java → JDK 25.0.1 (java2501)

commit.message:
feat: encrypt all metadata at rest via AES-256-GCM with HKDF per-file keys (#1203)

EncryptedFileIO: thin AES-256-GCM utility deriving per-file keys from
BMW_WAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY via HKDF-SHA256. Wire format: ENCF magic + nonce
+ ciphertext + tag. Backward compatible — detects plaintext files by
missing magic and passes through unchanged.

Encrypted storage points:
- WalSnapshot: snapshot files (keys + head pointers)
- WalTableKeyAllocator: sequence ID files
- WalDataProvider: ID maps (per entity) and schema JSON files
- SearchIndexManager: search index binary files
- DiskBufferedLogger: audit log lines (per-line encryption)

Not yet covered: LocalPagedFile data pages (needs page format redesign
for per-page nonce+tag overhead — tracked separately).

Closes #1203

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: Battery level indicator for Bluetooth devices (#62)

* feat: display battery level for Bluetooth devices

Read org.bluez.Battery1.Percentage via D-Bus in BluetoothManager monitoring
loops (both polling and signal-based). Expose battery_level in DeviceStatus
dataclass and /api/status response. Render battery badge (🔋/🪫) next to
device name in Web UI when the device reports battery data.

Devices without battery support (e.g. mains-powered speakers) show nothing.
